goal: Highest-standard Lean formal verification for Hegemon as a PQC privacy chain, with critical theorem targets proved and production-bound at little to no performance cost.
completion_method: Weighted average of property completion percentages. A property reaches 100 only when the top-level Lean theorem is proved, assumptions are explicit, production conformance/refinement gates bind the shipped path, formal-core includes the gate, and performance-sensitive runtime changes are benchmarked or demonstrably cheap.

live service height stayed at 11218 during a 20-second mining-status sample, so block production was confirmed through the isolated single-node mining test instead of claiming live height advancement

last_coordinator_audit.summary: Audits confirmed strong Lean hygiene, admission/vector framework, nullifier and balance guardrails, parser/canonical encoding evidence, replay/admission/network posture, and release gates. They also confirmed the highest-value gaps remain proof-system soundness, spend authorization, privacy/confidentiality games, bridge production mint safety, cryptographic primitive assumptions, and full Rust/native-node refinement.
critical_open_gaps: No end-to-end theorem that accepted spends imply authorized private witnesses.
No in-repo proof-system soundness theorem for the deployed proving stack or full AIR-to-spec equivalence.
No formal privacy, zero-knowledge, unlinkability, or note-ciphertext confidentiality game.
No complete Rust/native-node refinement from raw network/RPC bytes through storage, reorg, restart, and accepted state publication.
Bridge local replay binding is stronger than production mint safety; external proof soundness and release verifier posture remain limiting.

last_theorem_slice.summary: Strengthened the ciphertext privacy-game boundary with a packaged theorem that composes same-shape public ciphertext facts, public metadata equality, explicit batch/timing equality, raw-wire indistinguishability, and first-class proof-system zero-knowledge, wallet metadata, timing/batching, and network metadata assumptions. The theorem also proves observer, public metadata, and batch/timing projections ignore private witnesses and prover randomness once public wire data is fixed.

validation.lean_formal: targeted lake build Hegemon.Privacy.CiphertextPrivacy passed locally 2026-06-14T21:42:41Z; full check_lean_formal passed inside scripts/check_formal_core.sh locally 2026-06-14T22:04:06Z
validation.formal_core: bash scripts/check_formal_core.sh passed locally 2026-06-14T22:04:06Z with 100 claims, 1428 named Lean theorem declarations, 90 production-eligible claims, 389 blueprint edges, 413 falsification cases, and 200 implementation bindings

known_limitations: The theorem packages proof-system ZK, wallet metadata hygiene, timing/batching, network metadata, and raw-wire indistinguishability as explicit assumptions; it does not discharge them.
Batch/timing facts are modeled as explicit leakage, not hidden data.
This does not prove ML-KEM/AEAD/KDF/RNG security, simulator zero knowledge, proof-system privacy, wallet/network metadata privacy, timing privacy, or complete wallet/native-node equivalence.
The production cost is zero on the hot path; this adds Lean proof obligations and metadata only.

last_production_binding_slice.summary: Bound live hegemon_submitAction bridge ingress to the same Lean-backed bridge payload admission chain used by native block and action validation. Bridge RPC submissions now construct zero-delta pending actions, then run validate_action_state through validate_pending_action_against_mempool_state and validate_bridge_action_payload before action hashing, sled insertion, durability flush, or pending-map publication.
known_limitations: No new Lean theorem was added in this slice; it binds the existing bridge action payload admission and bridge mint-safety theorem families to live RPC bridge ingress.

The focused regression covers empty outbound payload and empty inbound receipt rejection before staging; replay, destination, payload-hash, and receipt-output regressions remain covered by the existing bridge validation tests and vector gates.
External-chain proof soundness, the future PQ-clean bridge mint grammar, bridge payload-hash security, and complete raw-byte native-node equivalence remain separate open work.
